data.title: Bitcoin set for weekly gain as cooler-than-expected CPI data offer brief boost
data.description:
Bitcoin (BTC-USD) extended its recent rally on Friday, putting the cryptocurrency on track for a weekly advance, and helping it claw back some of it's M/M decline. Softer-than-expected consumer inflation data offered a brief intraday boost

The price of bitcoin (BTC-USD) rose to as high as $111.8K less than a minute after the release of the delayed CPI report, before paring that increase to $111.3K, which was still up 1.2% in the last 24 hours.

In the past five trading sessions, the token advanced 4% at press time, narrowing its M/M loss to less than 1%. Bitcoin (BTC-USD) started the week ending Oct. 24 strong, as the risk-on mood improved across global equity markets. It dropped to as low as ~$104K last week, when financial markets broadly sold off amid trade tensions between the U.S. and China and concerns about the health of U.S. regional banks.

Earlier on Friday, the Bureau of Labor Statistics' Consumer Price Index -- which was delayed due to the ongoing government shutdown -- rose less than expected in September, at 0.3% M/M and 3.0% Y/Y. That's welcoming news for the Federal Reserve, as it's expected to cut interest rates again at its meeting next week. 

U.S. stock index futures also rallied after the CPI data.

Other cryptos on the move include: Ether (ETH-USD) +3%, ZCash (ZEC-USD) +10%, Story (IP-USD) +6.6%, Ethena (ENA-USD) +5.4%, Cronos (CRO-USD) +4.1%, XRP (XRP-USD) +3.9% and Stellar (XLM-USD) +3.2%.

data.title: Treasury yields sink as bonds rally after soft September consumer inflation report
data.description:
U.S. Treasury yields largely fell across the board as traders snapped up bonds after the September consumer price index ((CPI)) data came in cooler than expected. 

The benchmark 10-year Treasury yield (US10Y) was last down 2 basis points to 3.98%, while the shorter-end, more rate-sensitive 2-year yield (US2Y) was down 4 basis points to 3.47%.

Earlier, market participants starved of U.S. economic data due to the ongoing government shutdown received a reprieve in the form of the delayed CPI report. According to the U.S. Bureau of Labor Statistics, headline CPI came in at +0.3% M/M, versus an expected figure of +0.4%. Core CPI for September was +0.2% M/M, versus a consensus of +0.3%.

On a Y/Y basis, the core CPI reading of +3.0% was still well above the Federal Reserve's 2% target. Still, the soft monthly readings give the central bank more room to cut interest rates, which it is widely expected to do at its monetary policy committee meeting next week.

"In many ways it’s the perfect K-Shaped economic report. It gives cover to the Fed to cut in October & December as the monthly pace of inflation eased a bit and is up 3% from a year ago," Joseph Brusuelas, principal and chief economist at RSM US LLP, said on X (formerly Twitter). 

Here are some exchange-traded funds of interest that track the fixed-income markets: (NASDAQ:TLT), (NYSEARCA:TLH), (NASDAQ:IEF), (NASDAQ:IEI), (NASDAQ:SHY), (NYSEARCA:SGOV), (NYSEARCA:SCHO), (NYSEARCA:BIL), (NYSEARCA:AGG), (NASDAQ:BND), (NASDAQ:VCIT), (NYSEARCA:MUB), (NASDAQ:MBB), (NYSEARCA:JNK), (NYSEARCA:LQD), (NYSEARCA:HYG), and (NYSEARCA:TIP).

data.title: Allegiant September passenger traffic increases 1.4%
data.description:
•  Allegiant Travel Company (NASDAQ: ALGT) on Friday reported a 1.4% increase in passenger traffic in September nearly to 983,840, increasing the total number of miles flown by paying passengers (revenue passenger miles) by nearly 0.7% to 851,297.
  • Available seat miles rose 1.8% to 1.03M.
  • ALGT +1.71% premarket to $65.9.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Kodiak spikes as J.P. Morgan upgrades on eye disease drug, KSI-101
data.description:
Kodiak Sciences (NASDAQ:KOD) added ~7% in the premarket on Friday after J.P. Morgan upgraded the eye drug developer, arguing that its experimental therapy KSI-101, which is a bispecific protein targeting IL-6 and VEGF, has been derisked by a recent data readout from Roche (OTCQX:RHHBY).

Upgrading the stock to Overweight from Neutral, analyst Anupam Rama pointed to Kodiak’s (NASDAQ:KOD) Phase 3 PEAK and PINNACLE studies for KSI-101 in macular edema secondary to inflammation, which are expected to read out in Q4 2026 and Q1 2027, respectively.

Rama noted recent data Roche (OTCQX:RHHBY) disclosed from its MEERKAT and SANDCAT Phase 3 trials for its IL-6-targeting monoclonal antibody, vamikibart, in uveitic macular edema, a rare inflammatory eye disease.

“The recent competitive MEERKAT / SANDCAT updates for vamikibart underscore evidence that IL-6 is a viable mechanism in inflammatory ocular diseases,” Rama wrote, noting that the readout derisks KSI-101. The analyst raised his price target on KOD to $24 from $15 per share.

data.title: Soft retail inflation data supports Fed's easing cycle
data.description:
The cooler-than-expected September retail inflation report seems to have confirmed that the Federal Reserve will continue their easing cycle in their next meeting. 

Currently, the odds of the Fed slashing interest rates by 25 basis points stand at 98.9%, according to CME's FedWatch tool. 

"Inflation coming in weaker than expected further solidifies a continuation of the Fed's rate-cutting cycle, at least for the next two meetings.  The Fed have made up their minds that at least two Fed cuts are warranted as insurance against a weakening labor market and to appease the administration, which is watching over their every move," Skyler Weinand, chief investment officer, Regan Capital, said. 

The delayed CPI report finally landed and came in slightly cooler. CPI rose +0.3% M/M vs. 0.4% consensus and +0.4% in August. CPI rose +3.0% Y/Y vs. +3.1% consensus and +2.9% prior.

Core CPI came in at +0.2% M/M vs. +0.3% consensus and +0.3% prior, and Y/Y, +3.0% vs. +3.1% consensus and +3.1% prior.

The report was delayed due to the ongoing U.S. government shutdown.

Weinand expects at least two 25-basis-point cuts by the end of the year and sees the Fed taking a brief halt to review a few months of data before easing again in 2026.

B. Riley Wealth chief market strategist Art Hogan also believes that the report will clearly keep the Fed on track to cut rates at its next meeting on Oct. 29.  

"The Fed has been clear that they are more focused on the softening labor data and will continue to defend their full employment mandate, even with core CPI well above their 2% target,” Hogan added. 

Weinand continued that due to the lack of available data owing to the government shutdown, they expect the cuts to proceed. However, if the government reopens and the labor market reports show weakness, then Weinand expects either a 50 basis point cut for December or the Fed to communicate a string of cuts in 2026.

"Friday's CPI numbers, while delayed due to the government shutdown, are real and based on real data from September, however, the collection and reliability of the data continue to weaken.  The market will take alternative data sources more seriously going forward," Weinand said.

Stock index futures rose on Friday as the September consumer price index came in a little cooler than expected, while market sentiments also got a boost from Intel's ((INTC)) results.

Here are some exchange-traded funds that track the S&P 500: (NYSEARCA:SPY), (NYSEARCA:VOO), (NYSEARCA:IVV), (NYSEARCA:RSP), (NYSEARCA:SSO), (NYSEARCA:UPRO), (NYSEARCA:SH), (NYSEARCA:SDS), and (NYSEARCA:SPXU).

data.title: General Motors cuts 200 salaried staff at Michigan tech hub - Bloomberg
data.description:
General Motors (NYSE:GM) laid off more than 200 salaried employees on Friday in a move the automaker attributed to “business conditions” as the company steps up efforts to increase efficiencies at its corporate level, Bloomberg reported citing sources present at the corporate meeting. 

The majority of the cuts were at GM’s (NYSE:GM) Tech Center in Warren, Michigan, the company’s hub for autonomous development, software development, and new vehicle design.

This is the third time this year that GM (GM) has reduced its corporate staff with 1,000 salaried employees let go in January and another 1,000 in August. Through the company’s revamped performance review system, the bottom 5% of performers across the organization have been singled out for immediate termination this year.

GM’s (GM) third-quarter results showed ongoing challenges in its EV business and from import tariffs. Still, thanks to tariff-mitigation measures and increased U.S. production, the company lifted its full-year EPS guidance by about 11%.

data.title: EQT’s Mountain Valley Pipeline asks for FERC approval of MVP Boost project
data.description:
EQT-led (NYSE:EQT) Mountain Valley Pipeline said late Thursday it filed an application with the Federal Energy Regulatory Commission seeking authorization to build the "MVP Boost" project, which would add 600 million cf/day of available capacity to the interstate Appalachian natural gas line that began operations in June 2024.

Mountain Valley Pipeline said the upsized capacity is fully subscribed by investment-grade utilities in North Carolina and Virginia through binding, long-term contracts, highlighting strong, demand-driven growth for reliable natural gas supply.

The joint venture conducted an open season to gauge shipper interest in MVP Boost, which was initially anticipated to increase capacity on the MVP Mainline by 500,000 dth/day, leading to the request for additional capacity.

Pending regulatory approval, construction is targeted to start in the winter of 2026-27, with an in-service date targeted for mid-2028.

The Mountain Valley Pipeline JV is comprised of operator EQT Corp. (EQT), NextEra Energy (NEE), Consolidated Edison (ED), AltaGas (OTCPK:ATGFF) and RGC Resources (RGCO).

data.title: Coinbase raised to Overweight at J.P. Morgan on ability to monetize Base, UDC payouts
data.description:
Coinbase Global (NASDAQ:COIN) stock climbed 4.0% in Friday morning trading after J.P. Morgan upgraded the crypto exchange to Overweight from Neutral, citing it expectation the company will explore a Base token further explore USD Coin (USDC-USD) payouts.

Note, too, that crypto prices climbed after the delayed CPI data showed that inflation has cooled slightly, leading financial markets to expect another rate cut at the Federal Reserve's October 28-29 meeting.

Analyst Kenneth B. Worthington sees a $12B-$34B opportunity for Coinbase (NASDAQ:COIN) in launching a Base token, which he expects to accelerate the growth of development on the Base blockchain, with the added benefit of "enabling Coinbase to equitize the success in what is the largest L2 on multiple metrics."

Furthermore, experimenting with yields on USDC Rewards could drive further growth of Coinbase One and better monetize on-platform USDC, Worthington said.

The Overweight rating on Coinbase (NASDAQ:COIN) contrasts with the SA Quant rating and the average SA Analyst rating of Hold, and aligns with the average Wall Street rating of Buy.

data.title: 4 stocks to watch on Friday: TGT, PG, VLO, GD
data.description:
Stock index futures rose on Friday as the September consumer price index came in a little cooler than expected, while market sentiments also got a boost from Intel's ((INTC)) results. Here are four stocks to watch on the day: 

Target (TGT) shares were flat in premarket trade as it disclosed on Thursday that it will cut 1,800 positions from its corporate headcount. The specific employees being let go will not be notified until next Tuesday. The job slashing is the second-largest ever of Target's (NYSE:TGT) headquarters staff.

Procter & Gamble (PG) rose ~4% before the opening bell as the household products giant beat FQ1 earnings estimates, helped by a robust growth in its organic sales. Organic sales increased 2% in FQ1 to beat the consensus estimate of +1.4%. Notably, beauty segment organic sales increased 6% year-over-year.

Valero (VLO) was up 0.5% in premarket trade as it said it was moving forward with its plan to close its 145,000 bbl/day Benicia refinery in California by next spring after talks with state officials about options to keep it running. The California Energy Commission and state officials reportedly have been working to find a buyer for the Benicia refinery and exploring other options to keep the plant in operation.

General Dynamics (GD) shares were up 4.6% before the opening bell as the firm beat both its top and bottom line estimates in the third quarter. The company posted GAAP EPS of $3.88, beating estimates by $0.17, and revenue of $12.9 billion, up 10.6% Y/Y, and topping Wall Street consensus by $370 million.

data.title: MultiSensor AI Holdings announces pricing of $14M private placement
data.description:
• MultiSensor AI Holdings (NASDAQ:MSAI) on Friday reported that it has executed a securities purchase agreement to raise gross proceeds of approximately $14 million.
  • The initial closing of the offering, representing gross proceeds of approximately $2.8 million, is expected to close on October 27.
  • The balance of the offering is expected to close in December 2025.
